title: What A Boat Dealership Sales Portal Should Show
subheader: Helmful gives marine sales teams a clear view of leads, opportunities, estimates, contracts, transactions, invoices, and payments.
featuredImagePrompt: A marine dealership manager reviewing a sales dashboard on a desktop monitor, with salespeople talking near boats in a showroom. Clean realistic business photography, no text.
body:
A sales portal should answer daily dealership questions quickly.

How many leads came in? Which opportunities are active? Which estimates are waiting? What contracts were sent? Which deals turned into invoices and payments?

If managers cannot answer those questions from the system, they end up chasing updates from the team.

What Helmful Does

Helmful gives dealers a sales overview with connected records across leads, opportunities, estimates, contracts, transactions, invoices, and payments.

It also gives managers filters for period, salesperson, location, and custom date range, so the view matches how the store actually runs.

How It Helps

Managers can see pipeline activity without rebuilding reports. Salespeople can follow the customer from first lead to closed deal. The team has one process instead of separate tools for each stage.

That makes coaching easier and helps prevent quiet deals from slipping out of view.

What To Review Weekly

Start with new leads, open opportunities, stale estimates, contracts awaiting signature, and recent invoices or payments.

Helmful keeps those records connected, which makes the weekly sales meeting shorter and more useful.
